evidence: Absolute space strongly resonates with permanence (eternal existence), cosmic scale (universal scope), structural ontology (spatial framework), independence (self-contained), essential resonance (core essence of spatiality), predictable lawfulness (unchanging nature), and functional value (practical spatial extension). Scores reflect its foundational, unchanging nature contrasting with FND.spaceless.

Aesthetic value (8) arises from its association with modern design. Predictable (7) as a consistent spatial property, structural patterns (5), and functional utility (5) further align with its spatial essence.

Three-dimensional strongly resonates with perceptual awareness (epistemological.perception), material existence (ontological.material), spatial structure (ontological.structural), essential spatiality (resonance.essential), necessity (lawfulness.necessary), and functional utility (value_generation.functional). It also connects moderately with identification (epistemological.identification), comprehension (experience.comprehension), and aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic).

Topographic strongly resonates with spatial physicality (ontological.material 7), essential spatial essence (resonance.essential 7), and practical utility (value.functional 7). It also connects to spatial data structures (ontological.informational 5), societal scales (scale.society 2), and independent existence (interconnection.independent 5). Lower scores reflect its neutral stance on time, ethics, and cosmic scales.

Topographical strongly resonates with spatial structure (ontological.structural:3, ontological.material:5), functional utility (value.functional:5), and perception/experience of physical features (epistemological.perception:2, experience.comprehension:3). It weakly connects to societal scale (scale.society:1), causal relationships (interconnection.catalytic:1), and predictable patterns (lawfulness.predictable:3).

Topological space resonates strongly with structural and abstract concepts (ontological.structural=8, resonance.essential=6), as it defines spatiality through mathematical structure. It influences modern math (temporal.transformation=5) and requires deep understanding (epistemological.understanding=7). Its generative nature (symmetry.generative=4) and aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic=6) reflect its foundational role in abstract spatial reasoning.

Two-dimensional directly relates to spatial structure (ontological.structural), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and its application in functional contexts (e.g., blueprints) and aesthetic ones (e.g., art). Its lawful predictability stems from geometric rules.
